_about: Registry-owned security advisory feed. One JSON document containing all advisories; PRs append. Schema: schemas/security-advisory.schema.json. CI gate: registry/scripts/check-advisories.mjs. Runbook: docs/runbooks/INCIDENT-RESPONSE.md §Incident class 1.

advisories.summary: SSRF + DNS-rebinding gaps + empty-secret HMAC in campaign.sequence.webhook (1.0.0)
advisories.details:
The webhook executor in vendor.myndhyve.campaign-sequence-integration@1.0.0 had three composable defects:

1. SSRF deny list missed `172.16.0.0/12` (RFC 1918 private band) and all IPv6 private/link-local ranges (`fc00::/7`, `fe80::/10`). A workflow author could configure `webhookUrl` to hit internal services on those ranges.

2. DNS-rebinding bypass: the deny check ran on the URL hostname, not on the resolved IP. A public hostname pointed at a private IP via attacker-controlled DNS bypassed the check entirely.

3. Empty-secret HMAC: when `step.webhookSecret` was absent or empty, the executor still computed `hmac-sha256('', body)` and emitted the result as `X-MyndHyve-Signature`. Any third party who knows the body can forge this signature — receivers verifying the header have no actual sender-authentication guarantee.

All three are addressed in 1.0.1: deny list extended to all RFC 1918 + IPv6 private + 169.254/16 + 0.0.0.0/8, DNS-resolution check added via `dns.lookup`, empty-secret refused for non-localhost targets with explicit error.
